title: Why Abolitionists Are Going to Win
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman argues that the abolitionist movement against abortion will ultimately prevail not because of the talent of its leaders, but because of the moral weight of its truth claims against both pro-abortion depravity and pro-life political cowardice. He contends that suppression by better-funded incumbents and slander campaigns can only delay, not defeat, the spread of abolitionist ideas.
audiences: secular observer evaluating the movement's prospects
political opponent or establishment pro-life leader

questions.question: According to Silberman, who reignited the modern Abolitionist Movement and how?
questions.audience: secular observer evaluating the movement's prospects
questions.answer: Silberman credits Russell Hunter with reanimating the Garrisonian principles of immediatism and applying them to the fight against abortion roughly a decade before this article was written, which he describes as the spark that reignited abolitionism.
questions.quote: A decade ago, he reanimated the Garrisonian principles of immediatism, applied them to the fight against abortion, and reignited the Abolitionist Movement.

questions.question: If the movement has so many talented people, why does Silberman insist talent is not the reason it will win?
questions.answer: Silberman concedes that diverse gifts have grown the movement, but argues that talent alone cannot guarantee victory because many talented groups have failed; victory will instead come from the moral weight and justice of the abolitionist message itself.
questions.quote: After all, lots of groups of talented people have failed to achieve their objectives. **Abolition** is going to be achieved because of the weightiness of the truth abolitionists proclaim.

questions.question: How does Silberman characterize the pro-abortion 'choice' argument, and what does he say will end the practice?
questions.audience: secular observer evaluating the movement's prospects
questions.answer: He calls the 'choice' justification for killing preborn children 'unashamedly depraved' and predicts that sustained, faithful preaching by the Church about the horrors of abortion will eventually bring it to an end, even though nations can sink to such depravity for a time.
questions.quote: Nations can and do succumb to this level of depravity, but a relentless and “faithful delineation of the horrors” of abortion by the Church of the living God will bring its practice to an end.

questions.question: What is Silberman's case against the establishment pro-life position of submitting to Roe?
questions.answer: He labels unconditional submission to the Supreme Court 'unashamedly absurd' in light of 63+ million deaths, points out that even eight Republican-appointed justices upheld Roe in 1992, and argues that the recent June Medical Services v. Russo decision shows there is no overturn of Roe in sight.
questions.quote: The establishment pro-life claim that though 63+ million human beings and counting have been murdered by abortion, we must submit unconditionally to the supreme court’s bloody tyranny, is unashamedly absurd.

questions.question: What is the core abolitionist pronouncement that Silberman says cannot be denied forever?
questions.answer: He frames it as a two-part claim: that abortion is murder, and that it is sinful for any officeholder at any level to submit to Roe and thereby deliver children to abortionists. He admits this can be denied 'for a time' but not in perpetuity.
questions.quote: The pronouncements of the abolitionists –  that abortion is murder and that it is a sin for any office holder at any level to submit to the evil of *Roe* and thereby submit the children to the abortionists’ knife – cannot be seriously denied in perpetuity.

questions.question: What concrete obstacles does Silberman acknowledge abolitionists currently face in elections?
questions.answer: He admits incumbent pro-life politicians often have roughly seven times the funding of abolitionist challengers, that wealthy PACs spend tens of thousands more against abolitionists, and that opponents spread slander painting abolitionists as 'cultists' or 'violent thugs.'
questions.quote: For now, incumbent pro-life politicians with seven times as much money as their abolitionist challengers and wealthy PACs spending tens of thousands more on their behalf can survive reelection.

questions.question: What is Silberman's final reason for confidence that abolitionism will succeed?
questions.answer: He grounds confidence in the proposition that 'truth is weighty' and that if the abolitionist message reaches the masses (which he says it is), it is only a matter of time before equal protection for preborn human beings is established, provided abolitionists keep their hands to the plow.
questions.quote: If abolitionists keep their hands to the plow, it is only a matter of time before equal protection for preborn human beings is established.

questions.question: Whom does Silberman name as exemplary pastors and organizations within the movement?
questions.answer: He highlights pastors including Dusty Deevers, Thomas Lowery, Tim Gillespie, Brett Baggett, Sam Ketcher, Bill Ascol, Kelly Greene, and Chris Gore, alongside organizations like Abolish Abortion Texas, Apologia Studios, Operation Save America, Missionaries to the Preborn, and the state abolitionist societies.
questions.quote: Pastors like Dusty Deevers, Thomas Lowery, Tim Gillespie, Brett Baggett, Sam Ketcher, Bill Ascol, Kelly Greene, and Chris Gore are stellar expositors and practicers of God’s word.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Develops the Garrisonian principle of immediatism that Silberman credits Russell Hunter with reviving.

related.title: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
related.reason: This is the article Silberman links to when arguing that establishment pro-life strategy has failed and Roe will not be overturned.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the substantive critique of incremental pro-life lawmaking that underpins Silberman's dismissal of submission to Roe.

related.title: Why Do Pro-Lifers Despise Abolitionists?
related.reason: Explores the slander and opposition from pro-life incumbents that Silberman cites as a current obstacle for abolitionist candidates.

related.title: Does Romans 13 Really Instruct Christians To Allow the Killing of Babies?
related.reason: Addresses the theological objection to defying Roe that follows directly from Silberman's claim that submission to the court is sinful.

related.title: King Josiah’s Smashmouth Abolitionism
related.reason: Offers a biblical model for the kind of immediatist, no-compromise approach Silberman says is winning hearts.

related.title: Prominent Southern Baptist Voice Calls For Total Abolition
related.reason: Concrete evidence of the kind of spreading conviction among God's people that Silberman claims is making victory inevitable.
